engineGuide.h2: Toyota IQ Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Toyota IQ across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: Compact three-cylinder VVT-i unit powering entry-level IQ models; valued for economy but prone to EGR and throttle issues.
compatibleVariants: IQ, IQ 1.0 VVT-i, IQ2 1.0, IQ CYCLE 1.0, IQ 1.0 Stop/Start
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging causing rough idle (60k-90k miles)
Throttle body contamination leading to stalling
Intake manifold cracking on early units (P0171 codes)
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1KR-FE Toyota IQ engine replacement

entries.title: 1.33L Petrol
entries.history: Four-cylinder Dual VVT-i engine offering stronger performance for IQ2/IQ3 trims; more complex but generally reliable with proper maintenance.
compatibleVariants: IQ2 1.33, IQ3 1.33, IQ Collection 1.33, IQ 1.33 CVT, IQ 1.33 Manual
commonFailures: Intake manifold crack causing jerking acceleration (70k+ miles)
Timing chain stretch on higher-mileage units
Carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection variants)
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1NR-FE Toyota IQ engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: Turbocharged D-4D common-rail diesel offering strong torque and fuel economy; popular with high-mileage urban drivers but requires careful maintenance.
compatibleVariants: IQ 1.4 D-4D, IQ 1.4 D-4D Manual, IQ 1.4 D-4D Multidrive
commonFailures: Injector seal failure causing diesel knock and rough running
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey vehicles
EGR cooler clogging leading to reduced power

entries.title: Electric
entries.history: Purpose-built electric drive unit for the limited-run IQ EV; zero-emission urban mobility with battery-pack integration requiring specialist handling.
commonFailures: Battery cell degradation reducing range (80k+ miles)
Drive motor bearing wear on high-mileage units

commonProblems.h2: Toyota IQ Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Toyota IQ Engine Stalling - Why 1KR-FE Units Cut Out at Idle
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup on the electronic throttle body and idle air control valve disrupts airflow calibration, causing intermittent stalling when stopping or idling.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean throttle body and IACV, reset ECU adaptation values
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may recur if carbon buildup continues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new OEM throttle body assembly, recalibrate, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable solution if stalling is throttle-related only

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if stalling indicates deeper internal wear

problems.recommendation: For IQs under £3,000 value, start with a specialist throttle clean (£95-£180). If stalling persists or the engine has over 80k miles, a rebuilt 1KR-FE from £850 often represents better long-term value than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1KR-FE engine prices for your Toyota IQ

problems.h4: Toyota IQ Jerking Acceleration - Intake Manifold Failure on 1NR-FE
problems.rootCause: Plastic intake manifold develops hairline cracks at mounting points, creating unmetered air leaks that trigger lean codes (P0171) and cause jerking under load.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Apply high-temp sealant to crack points, replace gaskets
Fit new or refurbished intake manifold assembly, vacuum leak test
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable if engine internals are sound

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with revised manifold, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if jerking accompanies other symptoms

problems.recommendation: If your IQ3 is otherwise healthy and under 90k miles, a specialist manifold replacement (£420-£780) is cost-effective. For higher-mileage units or multiple fault codes, a rebuilt 1NR-FE from £1,100 eliminates recurrence risk.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 1NR-FE Toyota IQ engine replacement

problems.h4: Toyota IQ Timing Chain Noise - 1KR-FE Stretch on High Mileage
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain stretch, causing rattling on cold start and potential valve timing drift; ignored, this can lead to catastrophic interference failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner and guide rails, reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Risky if chain is already stretched; not recommended

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides, seals; precise timing setup
repairOptions.longevity: Viable preventative fix if caught before damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if rattling is loud or mileage exceeds 100k

problems.recommendation: A faint rattle on cold start at 80k miles may justify a timing kit (£520-£890). However, if the noise is pronounced or the IQ has over 100k miles, a rebuilt engine from £850 is often more economical than preventative chain work.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1KR-FE engine prices for your Toyota IQ

problems.h4: Toyota IQ Diesel Injector Failure - 1ND-TV Rough Running
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injector seals degrade, allowing diesel leakage into cylinders; causes rough idle, white smoke, and potential hydro-lock risk if severe.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, replace copper seals and washers, recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if injectors themselves are still functional

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Send injectors for specialist refurbishment, fit with new seals
repairOptions.longevity: Good option if engine compression is healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt diesel engine with upgraded injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if injector failure coincides with other wear

problems.recommendation: For IQ diesels under 90k miles with isolated injector issues, seal replacement (£190-£340) or refurbishment (£480-£820) offers good value. If the engine has high mileage or shows additional symptoms, a rebuilt 1ND-TV from £1,200 provides greater peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 1ND-TV Toyota IQ diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Toyota IQ Check Engine Light - Common Fault Codes Explained
problems.rootCause: Multiple potential triggers: P0171 (lean mixture from intake leak), P0300 (misfire from ignition components), P0401 (EGR flow insufficient), or P0011 (VVT timing correlation).

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: OBD2 scan, clear codes, replace spark plugs or air filter
Replace faulty sensor, EGR valve, or ignition coil based on diagnosis
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if fault is isolated to one component

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine addressing root cause, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if multiple codes indicate systemic wear

problems.recommendation: Start with a specialist diagnostic (£65-£150) to pinpoint the exact fault. For single-component issues on lower-mileage IQs, targeted repair is cost-effective. If multiple codes appear on a high-mileage engine, compare rebuilt engine quotes before committing to repeated fixes.
problems.cta: Get free Toyota IQ engine diagnostic quotes

problems.h4: Toyota IQ Oil Consumption - 1KR-FE Burning Oil Symptoms
problems.rootCause: Piston ring wear or valve stem seal degradation allows oil to enter combustion chambers, causing blue exhaust smoke and increased top-up frequency.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, inspect valve cover gasket and seals
repairOptions.longevity: May reduce consumption if issue is minor

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace valve stem seals, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if compression test confirms rings are sound

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded rings and seals, 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your IQ uses less than 1L per 2,000 miles, start with PCV and seal checks (£85-£160). For consumption over 1L/1,000 miles or blue smoke under acceleration, a rebuilt 1KR-FE from £850 is typically more economical than top-end engine work.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1KR-FE engine prices for your Toyota IQ

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Toyota IQ
finalCta.paragraph: The Toyota IQ's compact design makes engine access challenging, but our UK network of specialists simplifies replacement. Whether you need a reconditioned 1KR-FE, rebuilt 1NR-FE, or refurbished 1ND-TV, compare prices from vetted suppliers offering supply-only or full supply & fit. Every engine includes upgraded components addressing known failure points and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Avoid main dealer premiums and get back on the road with confidence.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Toyota IQ Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Toyota IQ and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Toyota IQ Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Toyota IQ engine type is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Toyota IQ Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Professionally cleaned, tested and refurbished IQ engines with worn parts replaced to restore reliable performance.
types.frontDescription: Professionally cleaned, tested and refurbished IQ engines with worn parts replaced to restore reliable performance.
types.frontDisclaimer: Prices vary by engine code and condition; supply only unless fitted service selected.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned IQ engines undergo thorough inspection, cleaning and replacement of worn components like seals, gaskets and bearings. Ideal for budget-conscious owners seeking reliable performance without premium rebuild costs.
backBullets: Suitable for IQs under 90k miles with no major internal damage
Typically 4-6 week turnaround from UK specialist workshops
Includes minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty through EngineMarket

types.title: Toyota IQ Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Comprehensive rebuild of your IQ engine with upgraded components addressing known failure points for enhanced longevity.
types.frontDescription: Comprehensive rebuild of your IQ engine with upgraded components addressing known failure points for enhanced longevity.
types.frontDisclaimer: Final price depends on engine code, damage assessment and selected upgrades.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt Toyota IQ engines are stripped to block, machined to tolerance and reassembled with new pistons, rings, bearings and updated parts targeting model-specific weaknesses like intake manifolds or timing components.
backBullets: Best value for IQs with 80k+ miles or multiple fault codes
Upgraded components reduce recurrence risk of common failures

types.title: Toyota IQ Used Engines

types.title: Toyota IQ Remanufactured Engines

types.frontDisclaimer: Premium option; lead times may apply for low-volume engine codes.
types.backDescription: Remanufactured Toyota IQ engines are rebuilt to OEM specifications using genuine or OEM-equivalent parts. The closest option to a brand-new engine without dealer pricing.

items.familiesLabel: Common Toyota IQ Petrol Engine Families
families: 1KR-FE - Entry-level 1.0L three-cylinder; high replacement demand due to EGR/throttle issues
1NR-FE - 1.33L four-cylinder Dual VVT-i; stronger performance for IQ2/IQ3 trims
knownFor: Excellent fuel economy for city driving (45-55 mpg real-world)
EGR valve and throttle body carbon buildup on 1KR-FE units
Intake manifold cracking on higher-mileage 1NR-FE engines
Generally robust with regular servicing and quality oil changes

families: 1ND-TV - 1.4L turbo D-4D; strong low-end torque but injector seal vulnerability
knownFor: Outstanding fuel economy (55-65 mpg real-world) for urban commuting

DPF regeneration challenges on vehicles used primarily for short trips
Robust bottom end when maintained with correct oil specifications

items.descriptor: The limited-run IQ EV offered zero-emission urban mobility with a purpose-built electric drive unit requiring specialist assessment for replacement enquiries.

Replacement options more limited than combustion engine variants

engineYears.h2: Toyota IQ Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Toyota IQ's UK production run (2008-2015), helping owners quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The IQ launched with the economical 1.0L 1KR-FE petrol engine, establishing the model's city-car credentials.
keyChanges: Toyota IQ launched in UK with 1.0L 1KR-FE petrol engine only
Compact three-cylinder VVT-i design prioritising fuel economy
Initial trim: IQ and IQ2 with manual transmission
knownFor: Exceptional urban fuel economy (45-50 mpg real-world)
Early units prone to throttle body calibration issues
Tight engine bay complicates DIY maintenance
Strong residual values for low-mileage examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2009 Toyota IQ replacement engines
years.ticker: Toyota IQ 1.0 engine stalling • Toyota IQ 1KR-FE throttle body • Toyota IQ 1.0 replacement cost

years.description: Toyota expanded the IQ range with the more powerful 1.33L petrol and efficient 1.4L diesel, broadening appeal beyond pure city use.
keyChanges: Introduction of 1.33L 1NR-FE Dual VVT-i petrol engine for IQ2/IQ3
Launch of 1.4L 1ND-TV D-4D diesel variant for high-mileage drivers
IQ3 trim added with enhanced equipment and 1.33L engine
Multidrive (CVT) option available on select petrol variants
knownFor: 1NR-FE offers noticeably stronger performance than 1.0L unit
1ND-TV diesel achieves 55-65 mpg but requires DPF-aware driving
Intake manifold cracking emerges as known issue on 1NR-FE
Diesel injector seal failures begin appearing on higher-mileage units
years.cta: Compare Toyota IQ 2009-2011 engine replacement prices
years.ticker: Toyota IQ 1.33 jerking acceleration • Toyota IQ 1.4 D-4D injector • Toyota IQ 1NR-FE intake manifold

years.description: Facelifted IQ models received minor styling updates and the addition of Stop/Start technology on select 1.0L petrol engines for improved urban efficiency.
keyChanges: Introduction of Stop/Start system on 1KR-FE engines (2012 onwards)
Minor exterior refresh: updated bumpers, new wheel designs
IQ CYCLE special edition launched with unique styling
Enhanced interior materials and equipment across range
mainEngines: 1KR-FE - 1.0L petrol with/without Stop/Start (IQ, IQ2, IQ CYCLE)
knownFor: Stop/Start systems generally reliable but require correct battery specification
Timing chain stretch begins appearing on high-mileage 1KR-FE units
1NR-FE intake manifold issues become more widely reported
Diesel DPF regeneration challenges on predominantly urban-use vehicles
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2013 Toyota IQ engine replacement
years.ticker: Toyota IQ 1.0 timing chain • Toyota IQ Stop/Start battery • Toyota IQ 1.33 P0171 code

years.preview: Final Production: IQ Collection & EV Variant
years.description: The IQ range concluded with premium Collection trim and the limited-run electric IQ EV, representing the model's technological peak before discontinuation.
keyChanges: IQ Collection trim launched with premium equipment and 1.33L engine
Limited-production IQ EV electric variant introduced (fleet-focused)
Final model year 2015; no direct successor announced
Enhanced standard equipment across remaining petrol variants
mainEngines: IQ EV Motor - Electric drive unit (IQ EV only)
knownFor: IQ Collection represents best-equipped and most refined IQ variant
IQ EV requires specialist assessment for drive unit or battery issues
Late-production petrol engines benefit from minor running improvements
Strong enthusiast following supports parts availability post-production
years.cta: Compare final-year Toyota IQ engine prices
years.ticker: Toyota IQ Collection engine • Toyota IQ EV battery • Toyota IQ 2015 replacement cost

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Toyota IQ was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Toyota IQ specialists.

faq.h2: Toyota IQ Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Toyota IQ engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Toyota IQ engines typically cost £850-£2,800 supply-only depending on engine code and condition. Supply & fit services add £450-£850. Get tailored quotes via our comparison tool.
keyPoints: 1KR-FE 1.0L petrol rebuilt: £850-£2,100 supply only
1NR-FE 1.33L petrol rebuilt: £1,100-£2,600 supply only
1ND-TV 1.4L diesel rebuilt: £1,200-£2,800 supply only
Supply & fit adds £450-£850 depending on workshop location
items.cta: Compare Toyota IQ engine replacement prices now

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Toyota IQ?
items.answer: Yes, if your IQ is otherwise sound and valued under £3,500. A rebuilt engine from £850 often costs less than vehicle depreciation, extending life affordably.
keyPoints: Toyota IQ market value typically £1,500-£4,000 depending on spec
Rebuilt engine cost often lower than vehicle depreciation
Supply & fit options avoid DIY complexity in tight engine bay
12-month warranty protects your investment post-replacement
items.cta: Find out if engine replacement makes sense for your IQ

items.question: What is the most reliable Toyota IQ engine?
items.answer: The 1.33L 1NR-FE petrol is generally considered the most robust IQ engine when maintained properly, though all three main engines can be reliable with correct servicing.
keyPoints: 1NR-FE 1.33L petrol: robust four-cylinder design, fewer inherent weaknesses
1KR-FE 1.0L: reliable but prone to EGR/throttle issues if neglected
1ND-TV 1.4L diesel: strong but requires DPF-aware driving patterns
Regular oil changes with correct specification critical for all variants
items.cta: Compare rebuilt engine options for your IQ engine code

items.question: What causes Toyota IQ engines to stall at idle?
items.answer: Stalling on 1KR-FE engines is usually caused by carbon buildup on the throttle body or idle air control valve disrupting airflow calibration at low RPM.
keyPoints: Carbon deposits accumulate on electronic throttle body over time
Idle air control valve can stick or become contaminated
ECU adaptation values may need resetting after cleaning
Specialist cleaning costs £95-£180 versus £850+ for engine replacement
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota IQ stalling repair or replacement

items.question: Can I replace a 1.0L IQ engine with a 1.33L unit?
items.answer: Technically possible but not recommended due to different mounting points, wiring looms, ECU calibration and transmission compatibility requiring extensive modification.
keyPoints: 1KR-FE and 1NR-FE have different engine mount locations
Wiring harnesses and ECU software are engine-code specific
Transmission bellhousing patterns may not match
Cost of conversion often exceeds value of a standard IQ
items.cta: Compare correct engine code replacements for your IQ

items.question: How long does a rebuilt Toyota IQ engine last?
items.answer: A properly rebuilt Toyota IQ engine with upgraded components addressing known failure points can reliably deliver 60,000-100,000+ miles with regular maintenance.
keyPoints: Quality rebuilds replace wear items: rings, bearings, seals, gaskets
Upgraded components target model-specific weaknesses
Regular oil changes with correct spec essential for longevity
12-month unlimited mileage warranty provides initial peace of mind
items.cta: Get quotes for long-life rebuilt Toyota IQ engines

items.question: Does a used Toyota IQ engine come with warranty?
items.answer: Most reputable UK suppliers offer minimum 3-month warranty on used IQ engines; EngineMarket network suppliers provide minimum 12-month coverage on rebuilt units.
keyPoints: Used engine warranties typically 3-6 months, parts only
Rebuilt/reconditioned engines often include 12-month unlimited mileage
Always confirm warranty terms, exclusions and claim process before purchase
Supply & fit services may include additional labour warranty
items.cta: Check warranty terms when comparing Toyota IQ engine quotes

items.question: What are common signs my Toyota IQ needs an engine replacement?
items.answer: Key indicators include persistent stalling, excessive oil consumption (>1L/1,000 miles), loud timing chain rattling, blue exhaust smoke, or multiple unresolved fault codes.
keyPoints: Blue smoke under acceleration suggests piston ring or valve seal wear
Loud rattling on cold start may indicate timing chain stretch
Oil consumption exceeding 1L per 1,000 miles signals internal wear
Multiple fault codes with poor performance warrant professional assessment
items.cta: Get a specialist diagnostic quote for your Toyota IQ

items.question: Can I fit a Toyota IQ engine myself?
items.answer: Possible for experienced mechanics but not recommended due to the IQ's extremely tight engine bay requiring specialist tools and knowledge of compact-car procedures.
keyPoints: Toyota IQ engine bay is among the tightest in its class
Access to mounts, wiring and ancillaries requires specific techniques
ECU adaptation and immobiliser pairing need diagnostic equipment
Supply & fit services from £450 often represent better value than DIY risk
items.cta: Find Toyota IQ engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: How do I find the engine code on my Toyota IQ?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on the engine block (visible near the gearbox junction) and listed on your V5C registration document under "Engine number" or "Fuel type" details.
keyPoints: Common codes: 1KR-FE (1.0L petrol), 1NR-FE (1.33L petrol), 1ND-TV (1.4L diesel)
V5C document lists engine capacity and fuel type for verification
DVLA lookup via registration confirms exact variant and engine spec
Enter your reg on EngineMarket to auto-identify compatible engines
items.cta: Instantly identify your Toyota IQ engine code with our reg check
